bins.name: Expedited path for flexible loads
bins.desc: The Coalition supports expediting interconnection for curtailable and dispatchable large loads and hybrid facilities. It argues storage is the fastest way to make loads flexible and that storage-backed loads can interconnect with minimal system impact, justifying faster study timelines.
quotes: The ANOPR’s proposal to expedite interconnection studies for large loads and hybrid facilities that agree to be curtailable and dispatchable can, if implemented effectively, significantly increase the pace of interconnection for large loads and their associated supply resources.
Energy storage is uniquely positioned as a rapid pathway for making loads curtailable and flexible, enabling them to potentially seek and obtain expedited interconnection.

Enabling large loads to avoid drawing from the grid by maintaining dispatchable energy during peak demand events—when transmission constraints are most salient—can dramatically reduce or eliminate the need for network upgrades that would otherwise require years of study and construction (as well as sizeable costs) to accommodate.

the Commission should ensure in any final action that storage resources are permitted to charge in low-usage intervals, when doing so will not trigger the need for expensive and long-lead-time upgrades that could easily place loads behind their planned interconnection schedules.

These limited withdrawal rights should be applicable in defined, constrained intervals, that will enable flexibility while also providing clarity regarding which constraints are binding, and which actions are needed to avoid any adverse impacts.

On the load side, major considerations include the percentage of load that must be curtailable, how many hours annually a load must be flexible, what response times are required, and whether curtailability commitments must be firm or may be conditional on operational constraints.
Without objective criteria, transmission providers will apply inconsistent standards, and developers will face regulatory uncertainty that undermines the benefits of expedited processing.

bins.name: Realistic study assumptions for storage
bins.desc: The filer wants load and supply studied together but warns against modeling storage with unrealistic charging assumptions that would lengthen studies. It points to Order No. 2023's intended-use modeling as a workable precedent for hybrid interconnection studies.
quotes: The ANOPR’s proposal to expedite interconnection studies for large loads and hybrid facilities that agree to be curtailable and dispatchable can, if implemented effectively, significantly increase the pace of interconnection for large loads and their associated supply resources.

if storage resources are studied using unrealistic assumptions (for instance, that they would opt to charge in congested, high-priced grid intervals), the resulting unnecessarily lengthy study processes could limit load’s ability to benefit from this quickly-deployable asset.
The Commission’s requirement in Order No. 2023 that storage be modeled based upon intended use sets a clear pathway for hybrid interconnection as well.

bins.name: Storage avoids network upgrade costs
bins.desc: The Coalition argues storage-backed load flexibility during peak events can reduce or eliminate the need for costly, multi-year network upgrades.
quotes: Enabling large loads to avoid drawing from the grid by maintaining dispatchable energy during peak demand events—when transmission constraints are most salient—can dramatically reduce or eliminate the need for network upgrades that would otherwise require years of study and construction (as well as sizeable costs) to accommodate.

bins.name: Adjacent paired resources for flexibility
bins.desc: The filer asks FERC to allow flexibility commitments to be met by resources physically or electrically adjacent to the load, and cites SPP's substation-distance presumption as a useful model for clear pairing rules.
quotes: The Commission should also ensure that load commitments to flexibility can be met by resources physically and/or electrically adjacent to load.

SPP’s recent High Impact Large Load proposal would enable expedited interconnection for load paired with generation, if the resources are within two substations of one another, or one substation if the transmission facility between them is 765kV.

bins.name: Include storage in hybrid facilities
bins.desc: The Coalition urges FERC to specify that storage resources count as part of a load-supply hybrid and to mirror the generator procedures that already accommodate storage. It also wants hybrid facilities to have a means of expediting interconnection and storage charging rights protected.
quotes: The ANOPR’s proposal to expedite interconnection studies for large loads and hybrid facilities that agree to be curtailable and dispatchable can, if implemented effectively, significantly increase the pace of interconnection for large loads and their associated supply resources.
As a threshold matter, the Commission should specify that storage resources can be considered as part of a load-supply hybrid.
just as the Commission’s Large Generator Interconnection Procedures accommodate energy storage, its definition of “hybrid facilities” in the large load context should also include energy storage.
The Commission should also ensure that load commitments to flexibility can be met by resources physically and/or electrically adjacent to load.

the Commission should ensure in any final action that storage resources are permitted to charge in low-usage intervals, when doing so will not trigger the need for expensive and long-lead-time upgrades that could easily place loads behind their planned interconnection schedules.

bins.name: Storage enables expedited interconnection
bins.desc: The filer supports an expedited interconnection pathway and argues energy storage is uniquely positioned to make loads curtailable and flexible enough to qualify for it.
quotes: The ANOPR’s proposal to expedite interconnection studies for large loads and hybrid facilities that agree to be curtailable and dispatchable can, if implemented effectively, significantly increase the pace of interconnection for large loads and their associated supply resources.
Energy storage is uniquely positioned as a rapid pathway for making loads curtailable and flexible, enabling them to potentially seek and obtain expedited interconnection.

bins.name: Reduce upgrade cost via flexibility
bins.desc: The Coalition argues maintaining dispatchable storage energy during peaks can dramatically reduce or eliminate the network upgrade costs that would otherwise be required to serve large loads.
quotes: Enabling large loads to avoid drawing from the grid by maintaining dispatchable energy during peak demand events—when transmission constraints are most salient—can dramatically reduce or eliminate the need for network upgrades that would otherwise require years of study and construction (as well as sizeable costs) to accommodate.

bins.name: Objective curtailability criteria needed
bins.desc: The filer identifies open questions on how much load must be curtailable, how many hours, what response times, and whether commitments are firm. It urges objective criteria to avoid inconsistent standards and regulatory uncertainty, and asks FERC to account for regional differences.
quotes: On the load side, major considerations include the percentage of load that must be curtailable, how many hours annually a load must be flexible, what response times are required, and whether curtailability commitments must be firm or may be conditional on operational constraints.
Without objective criteria, transmission providers will apply inconsistent standards, and developers will face regulatory uncertainty that undermines the benefits of expedited processing.
The Commission should also be open considering and taking stock of regional differences on these issues.

bins.name: SPP High Impact Large Load model
bins.desc: The Coalition cites SPP's High Impact Large Load proposal, which would allow expedited interconnection for load paired with generation within two substations (or one at 765kV), as a helpful example of a proximity presumption.
quotes: SPP’s recent High Impact Large Load proposal would enable expedited interconnection for load paired with generation, if the resources are within two substations of one another, or one substation if the transmission facility between them is 765kV.

bins.name: Storage in hybrid definition
bins.desc: A core ask is that FERC's definition of hybrid facility in the large load context explicitly include energy storage, consistent with how the Large Generator Interconnection Procedures already accommodate storage.
quotes: As a threshold matter, the Commission should specify that storage resources can be considered as part of a load-supply hybrid.
just as the Commission’s Large Generator Interconnection Procedures accommodate energy storage, its definition of “hybrid facilities” in the large load context should also include energy storage.

bins.name: Defined storage charging rights
bins.desc: The filer asks that storage be permitted to charge in low-usage, defined and constrained intervals without triggering expensive upgrades, that charging be studied alongside injections, and that studies avoid unrealistic charging assumptions.
quotes: the Commission should ensure in any final action that storage resources are permitted to charge in low-usage intervals, when doing so will not trigger the need for expensive and long-lead-time upgrades that could easily place loads behind their planned interconnection schedules.

These limited withdrawal rights should be applicable in defined, constrained intervals, that will enable flexibility while also providing clarity regarding which constraints are binding, and which actions are needed to avoid any adverse impacts.

if storage resources are studied using unrealistic assumptions (for instance, that they would opt to charge in congested, high-priced grid intervals), the resulting unnecessarily lengthy study processes could limit load’s ability to benefit from this quickly-deployable asset.
The Commission’s requirement in Order No. 2023 that storage be modeled based upon intended use sets a clear pathway for hybrid interconnection as well.

bins.name: Accredit long-duration storage
bins.desc: The Coalition flags that no organized market accredits batteries beyond 8 hours and urges FERC to ensure grid operators develop capacity accreditation values for long-duration, multi-day, and hybrid storage resources to support large load interconnection.
quotes: certain energy storage and hybrid resources are not accredited in capacity markets today. The Commission should ensure that all grid operators develop capacity accreditation values for long-duration energy storage (>10 hours in duration), multi-day energy storage (>24 hours in duration), and hybrid resources that include various combinations of storage and generation.
No organized market today has accredited battery durations beyond 8 hours.
